outcomes.name: filepart_with_content
outcomes.description: FilePart {uri,language_id,content} embedded — does the model see file body?
notes: shibboleth 'XYZZY_TOKEN_42' found → model saw file content

outcomes.name: filepart_uri_only
outcomes.description: FilePart {uri} only, NO content — does Sourcegraph fetch via gitserver?
outcomes.request_preview: {"model": "anthropic::2024-10-22::claude-sonnet-4-5-latest", "messages": [{"speaker": "human", "content": [{"type": "file", "file": {"uri": "https://sourcegraph.com/github.com/sourcegraph/cody/-/blob/lib/shared/src/sourcegraph-api/completions/types.ts", "language_id": "typescript"}}, {"type": "text", "text": "I attached a file by URI only. Briefly describe what's in it. If you can't see the content, say 'no content visible'."}]}], "maxTokensToSample": 300, "temperature": 0, "stream": true, "topK…
outcomes.reply_preview:
I can see the file URI you've provided, which points to a TypeScript file at:

`github.com/sourcegraph/cody/-/blob/lib/shared/src/sourcegraph-api/completions/types.ts`

However, **no content visible** - the `<CONTEXT_FILE>` tag is empty and doesn't contain the actual file contents. I can only see th…
notes: Model said it can't see content → server does NOT auto-fetch from URI

outcomes.name: repopart
outcomes.description: RepoPart {name='github.com/sourcegraph/cody'} — does model get repo context?
outcomes.request_preview: {"model": "anthropic::2024-10-22::claude-sonnet-4-5-latest", "messages": [{"speaker": "human", "content": [{"type": "repo", "repo": {"name": "github.com/sourcegraph/cody", "filePatterns": ["README.*", ".*\\.md$"]}}, {"type": "text", "text": "I attached a repo. In one sentence, what is this repository primarily about? If you have no info on it, say 'no repo context'."}]}], "maxTokensToSample": 200, "temperature": 0, "stream": true, "topK": -1, "topP": -1}
